_comment: Per-feature, per-phase thresholds for non-gravid uterine EMG. Based on Lucovnik 2011, Bulletti 2000, Devedeux 1993, Taggart 1997, Khalil 2000, Horowitz 1989. Each feature has 4 phase ranges + a verdict text + clinical reasoning.

features.name: Contraction duration
features.patient_explanation: Period contractions last longer because your uterus is doing real work to shed its lining.
features.doctor_explanation: Sustained contraction durations of 8-30s are characteristic of menstrual myometrial activity (Lucovnik 2011). Shorter bursts are typical of non-gravid quiescence.

features.name: Signal strength (RMS)
features.patient_explanation: Stronger RMS = more powerful contraction. Period contractions are usually the strongest because hormones make your uterus work harder.
features.doctor_explanation: RMS > 25 uV indicates strong myometrial recruitment, consistent with active menstruation (Horowitz 1989). Values < 12 uV suggest follicular quiescence.

features.name: Peak amplitude
features.patient_explanation: Peaks > 60 uV usually mean menstrual contractions. Below 30 uV means your uterus is calm.
features.doctor_explanation: Peak amplitude > 60 uV correlates with internal uterine pressure during menstruation (Khalil 2000, r=0.82).

features.name: Peak-to-peak swing
features.patient_explanation: Wider swings mean more vigorous contractions.
features.doctor_explanation: Peak-to-peak voltage scales with myometrial cell synchrony and active fiber count.

features.name: Amplitude variability
features.patient_explanation: More variability means the contractions are powerful and changing — typical of period day.
features.doctor_explanation: Increased amplitude std reflects active recruitment/derecruitment cycles during menstrual contractions.

features.name: Dominant frequency
features.doctor_label: Peak PSD frequency in 0.1-3 Hz band
features.patient_explanation: Period contractions have a heartbeat-like rhythm around 0.5 Hz (one every 2 seconds).
features.doctor_explanation: Menstrual contractions concentrate spectrally at 0.4-0.9 Hz, the mechanical frequency of myometrial activity (Lucovnik 2011, Devedeux 1993).

features.name: Total power (0.1-3 Hz)
features.doctor_label: Integrated PSD power in EUG band
features.patient_explanation: Higher total power = more uterine action.
features.doctor_explanation: Total band power reflects aggregate myometrial energy expenditure.

features.name: Rhythmic power ratio
features.patient_explanation: Higher ratio means clockwork-like rhythm. Period contractions tick like a metronome.
features.doctor_explanation: Rhythmic concentration in 0.3-1 Hz sub-band quantifies contractile organization (Lucovnik 2011).

features.name: Cross-channel sync
features.patient_explanation: When all 3 sensors see the same contraction, it's a real one. Higher = more real.
features.doctor_explanation: Cross-channel sync > 0.4 indicates coordinated myometrial wavefront propagation, typical of menstrual labor-like activity (Rabotti 2010).

features.name: Regularity (CV)
features.patient_label: How evenly spaced the contractions are
features.doctor_label: Coefficient of variation of inter-contraction intervals
features.patient_explanation: Lower CV = contractions evenly spaced. Period contractions are like clockwork.
features.doctor_explanation: CV < 0.5 reflects rhythmic prostaglandin-driven uterine activity (Bulletti 2000, Taggart 1997, p<0.01).

features.name: Heart rate during contraction
features.patient_explanation: HR rises slightly during period contractions because your body is working harder.
features.doctor_explanation: Sympathetic activation during menstrual contractions raises HR by 5-10 bpm above baseline.

features.name: Heart rate rise
features.patient_label: How much your pulse jumps during contraction
features.patient_explanation: If your heart speeds up when a contraction happens, your body is responding — typical of period.
features.doctor_explanation: HR rise >= 2 bpm validates contraction as physiologically significant, supporting EUG detection (FYP pipeline).

features.name: Contractions per minute
features.patient_explanation: Period days: a contraction every 1-3 minutes. Other days: very few or none.
features.doctor_explanation: 0.3-1.2/min is consistent with active menstrual phase activity (Bulletti 2000).

features.name: Channel balance
features.patient_label: How evenly the 3 sensors picked up signal
features.patient_explanation: If all 3 sensors show similar strength, the signal is truly from your uterus, not noise.
features.doctor_explanation: Balanced channel amplitudes indicate signal originates from a centrally-located source (myometrium) rather than localized motion artifact.

MENSTRUAL.description: Days 1-5: Active menstrual contractions. Strong, rhythmic, coordinated.

FOLLICULAR.description: Days 6-13: Quiescent uterus preparing for ovulation. Weak, sporadic activity.

OVULATION.description: Day 14: Egg release. Brief mid-cycle uterine activity.

LUTEAL.description: Days 15-28: Post-ovulatory hormonal preparation. Variable, low-amplitude.
